tools.name: health_check
tools.description: Check if the Canvas API is reachable and the token is valid. Returns ok/error status.

tools.name: list_courses
tools.description: List courses for the authenticated user. Use fields="slim" when enumerating courses for selection; switch to "full" once you have identified the target. slim returns id, name, course_code, term name, and workflow_state only. "full" (default) includes all Canvas fields plus `include` extras (teachers, total_students, syllabus_body, etc.). `state[]` narrows by course workflow state; `enrollment_state` narrows by the caller's enrollment state.

tools.name: get_course
tools.description: Get details for a single course. Defaults to requesting `term` and `total_students`. Pass `include` to replace the default set with custom Canvas include[] fields (teachers, permissions, syllabus_body, sections, etc.).

tools.name: get_syllabus
tools.description: Get the syllabus HTML body for a course.

tools.name: create_course
tools.description: Create a new course in a Canvas account. Returns the created course object.

tools.name: update_course
tools.description: Update an existing course. Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields are left as-is.

tools.name: list_assignments
tools.description: List all assignments in a course. Use fields="slim" when enumerating assignments for selection; switch to "full" once you've identified the target. slim returns id, name, due_at, points_possible, published, and course_id only. "full" (default) includes all Canvas fields plus `include` extras (submission, all_dates, overrides, score_statistics, etc.). Use `bucket` to filter by past/upcoming/overdue/etc. Other filters: `search_term`, `assignment_ids`, `order_by`.

tools.name: get_assignment
tools.description: Get details for a single assignment by ID. Use `include` to request submission, overrides, all_dates, score_statistics, and other optional fields.

tools.name: list_assignment_groups
tools.description: List assignment groups (categories like Homework, Exams) in a course. Use `include=assignments` to nest assignments under each group; other includes add submission, overrides, score_statistics, etc.

tools.name: create_assignment
tools.description: Create a new assignment in a course.

tools.name: update_assignment
tools.description: Update an existing assignment in a course.

tools.name: delete_assignment
tools.description: Delete an assignment from a course. This action is permanent.

tools.name: list_submissions
tools.description: List all submissions for an assignment. Use `include` to attach user, assignment, rubric_assessment, submission_history, or visibility. Filter with `student_ids`, `workflow_state`, or `grading_period_id`. Defaults to including submission_comments.

tools.name: get_submission
tools.description: Get a single submission for a specific user on an assignment. Defaults to including submission_comments. Pass `include` to add rubric_assessment, submission_history, visibility, course, user, or read_status.

tools.name: grade_submission
tools.description: Post or update a grade for a submission. Requires grading permissions.

tools.name: comment_on_submission
tools.description: Add a text comment to a submission.

tools.name: list_rubrics
tools.description: List all rubrics in a course.

tools.name: get_rubric
tools.description: Get details for a single rubric by ID, including criteria.

tools.name: get_rubric_assessment
tools.description: Get the rubric assessment for a specific student submission on an assignment.

tools.name: submit_rubric_assessment
tools.description: Submit a rubric assessment with scores and comments for each criterion.

tools.name: create_rubric
tools.description: Create a new rubric in a course with criteria and rating levels. Optionally link it to an assignment immediately.

tools.name: list_quizzes
tools.description: List all quizzes in a course.

tools.name: get_quiz
tools.description: Get details for a single quiz by ID.

tools.name: list_quiz_submissions
tools.description: List all submissions for a quiz.

tools.name: list_quiz_questions
tools.description: List all questions in a quiz.

tools.name: get_quiz_submission_answers
tools.description: Get a student's answers for a quiz submission.

tools.name: score_quiz_question
tools.description: Score a specific question in a quiz submission. Specify attempt to score a particular attempt (omit for latest). Requires grading permissions.

tools.name: get_quiz_submission_events
tools.description: Get the event log for a Classic Quiz submission in chronological order. Events include session_started, question_answered, question_flagged, page_blurred, and page_focused. Use this to understand the timeline of a student's attempt. Classic Quizzes only — New Quizzes does not expose event logs via the Canvas REST API. Events are scoped to a single submission; Canvas enforces access permissions (instructors and the submitting student only). Do not use event logs as the sole basis for academic-integrity conclusions; present them with context.

tools.name: create_new_quiz
tools.description: Create a New Quiz (LTI) in a Canvas course. New Quizzes is the modern quiz engine; for Classic quizzes use create_quiz.

tools.name: update_new_quiz
tools.description: Update an existing New Quiz (LTI) in a Canvas course.

tools.name: delete_new_quiz
tools.description: Delete a New Quiz (LTI) from a Canvas course. This action is permanent. Use assignment_id (not quiz_id).

tools.name: list_new_quiz_items
tools.description: List all items (questions) in a New Quiz (LTI).

tools.name: get_new_quiz_item
tools.description: Get a single item (question) from a New Quiz (LTI) by item ID.

tools.name: create_new_quiz_item
tools.description: Create an item (question) in a New Quiz (LTI). Supports 5 types: choice (MCQ), true-false, essay, matching, numeric. Canvas may rate-limit rapid sequential creates. Call serially (not in parallel). For >50 items, chunk and pause between batches.

tools.name: update_new_quiz_item
tools.description: Update an existing item (question) in a New Quiz (LTI). All fields are optional; supply only what changes. Canvas may rate-limit rapid sequential updates. Call serially (not in parallel). For >50 items, chunk and pause between batches.

tools.name: delete_new_quiz_item
tools.description: Delete an item (question) from a New Quiz (LTI). This action is permanent.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List all files in a course.

tools.name: list_folders
tools.description: List all folders in a course.

tools.name: get_file
tools.description: Get metadata for a single file by ID, including download URL.

tools.name: upload_file
tools.description: Upload a file to a course. Content must be base64-encoded. Canvas performs a multi-step upload internally.

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file by ID. This action is permanent.

tools.name: download_file
tools.description: Download the content of a Canvas file by ID. Text files (plain text, HTML, JSON, XML, JavaScript) are returned as readable text. Binary files (images, PDFs, etc.) are returned as base64-encoded data. Files larger than 10 MB are refused.

tools.name: list_gradebook_history_days
tools.description: List the dates in a course gradebook history that contain grading activity, grouped by grader and assignment.

tools.name: get_gradebook_history_day
tools.description: Get the graders and assignment IDs that had gradebook activity on a specific course date.

tools.name: list_gradebook_history_submissions
tools.description: List versioned submission history for one grader and assignment on a specific gradebook history date.

tools.name: get_gradebook_history_feed
tools.description: Get the paginated gradebook history feed for a course, optionally filtered by assignment or user and optionally sorted oldest-first.

tools.name: list_students
tools.description: List all students enrolled in a course.

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Get details for a single user by ID.

tools.name: get_profile
tools.description: Get the profile of the currently authenticated user.

tools.name: search_users
tools.description: Search for users in a Canvas account by name, login, or email. Use `include` to request email, last_login, avatar_url, time_zone, or uuid in the response.

tools.name: list_course_users
tools.description: List users in a course with optional Canvas filters. Use `include` to request email, enrollments, avatar_url, bio, and other fields otherwise omitted from the default response. Use `enrollment_type` / `enrollment_state` to narrow by role or status, `search_term` to filter by name/login, `user_ids` to fetch a specific subset, and `sort`/`order` to control ordering.

tools.name: list_groups
tools.description: List all groups in a course.

tools.name: list_group_members
tools.description: List all members of a group.

tools.name: list_enrollments
tools.description: List all enrollments for the authenticated user across courses. Optional filters and includes mirror Canvas `GET /users/self/enrollments`.

tools.name: list_course_enrollments
tools.description: List enrollments within a specific course with Canvas filters. Use `include=grades` / `include=current_points` for richer grade data, `type[]` to limit to a role, and `user_id` to focus on a single user.

tools.name: enroll_user
tools.description: Enroll a user in a course with a specified role.

tools.name: remove_enrollment
tools.description: Remove or conclude an enrollment from a course. The 'delete' task is permanent; 'conclude' and 'deactivate' are reversible.

tools.name: list_discussions
tools.description: List all discussion topics in a course.

tools.name: get_discussion
tools.description: Get details for a single discussion topic by ID.

tools.name: list_announcements
tools.description: List all announcements in a course.

tools.name: post_discussion_entry
tools.description: Post a new entry (reply) to a discussion topic.

tools.name: create_discussion
tools.description: Create a new discussion topic in a course.

tools.name: update_discussion
tools.description: Update an existing discussion topic.

tools.name: delete_discussion
tools.description: Delete a discussion topic from a course. This action is permanent.

tools.name: list_modules
tools.description: List all modules in a course.

tools.name: get_module
tools.description: Get details for a single module by ID.

tools.name: list_module_items
tools.description: List all items within a module.

tools.name: get_course_structure
tools.description: Return the full module → items tree for a course in a single call, with summary stats. Avoids N+1 round-trips when an agent needs to reason over the whole course shape.

tools.name: view_course_structure
tools.description: Interactive tree view of a course's modules and items. Returns the same payload as `get_course_structure` and additionally links to an MCP Apps UI resource that renders an explorable tree with type filters and search. Hosts that do not support MCP Apps fall back to the JSON payload (same as `get_course_structure`).

tools.name: create_module
tools.description: Create a new module in a course.

tools.name: update_module
tools.description: Update an existing module (rename, reposition, publish/unpublish).

tools.name: create_module_item
tools.description: Add an item (Assignment, Page, Quiz, File, Discussion, ExternalUrl, ExternalTool, SubHeader) to a module.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: List all wiki pages in a course.

tools.name: get_page
tools.description: Get a single wiki page by its URL slug.

tools.name: create_page
tools.description: Create a new wiki page in a course.

tools.name: update_page
tools.description: Update an existing wiki page.

tools.name: delete_page
tools.description: Delete a wiki page from a course. This action is permanent.

tools.name: list_calendar_events
tools.description: List calendar events for a course.

tools.name: create_calendar_event
tools.description: Create a new calendar event in Canvas.

tools.name: update_calendar_event
tools.description: Update an existing calendar event. Only provided fields are changed.

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.description: List conversations (inbox messages) for the authenticated user.

tools.name: get_conversation
tools.description: Get a single conversation with its full message thread.

tools.name: get_conversation_unread_count
tools.description: Get the number of unread conversations for the authenticated user.

tools.name: send_conversation
tools.description: Send a new conversation message to one or more recipients.

tools.name: list_peer_reviews
tools.description: List all peer reviews for an assignment in a course.

tools.name: get_submission_peer_reviews
tools.description: List peer reviews assigned to a specific submission.

tools.name: create_peer_review
tools.description: Assign a user to peer-review a submission.

tools.name: delete_peer_review
tools.description: Remove a peer review assignment from a submission. This action is permanent.

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Get details for a Canvas account by ID.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List all accounts accessible to the authenticated user.

tools.name: list_sub_accounts
tools.description: List sub-accounts under a given Canvas account.

tools.name: list_account_courses
tools.description: List courses under a given Canvas account.

tools.name: list_account_users
tools.description: List users in a Canvas account.

tools.name: get_account_reports
tools.description: List available report types for a Canvas account.

tools.name: list_account_notifications
tools.description: List active global institution-wide announcements for the current user (maintenance windows, term deadlines, policy notices).

tools.name: view_account_notifications
tools.description: Interactive panel of active institution-wide announcements. Returns the same payload as `list_account_notifications` and additionally links to an MCP Apps UI resource that renders scannable announcement cards with type filters and search. Hosts that do not support MCP Apps fall back to the JSON payload (same as `list_account_notifications`).

tools.name: search_course_content
tools.description: Search for content within a course. Searches pages, assignments, discussions, and announcements by keyword.

tools.name: get_course_analytics
tools.description: Get course-level activity analytics. Returns daily page view and participation counts.

tools.name: get_student_analytics
tools.description: Get per-student activity analytics for a course. Returns page views, participations, and submission timeline for a specific student.

tools.name: get_course_activity_stream
tools.description: Get a summary of recent activity in a course. Returns counts of recent events grouped by type (submissions, discussions, announcements, etc.).

tools.name: get_root_outcome_group
tools.description: Get the root outcome group for an account or course context.

tools.name: list_outcome_groups
tools.description: List all outcome groups for an account or course context.

tools.name: list_outcome_group_links
tools.description: List all outcome links in an account or course context.

tools.name: get_outcome_group
tools.description: Get details for a specific outcome group in an account or course context.

tools.name: list_outcome_group_outcomes
tools.description: List the linked outcomes directly under a specific outcome group.

tools.name: list_outcome_group_subgroups
tools.description: List the immediate child outcome groups under a specific outcome group.

tools.name: get_outcome
tools.description: Get the full details for a specific learning outcome by ID.

tools.name: get_outcome_alignments
tools.description: Get outcome alignments for a course, optionally filtered to a specific student or assignment.

tools.name: get_outcome_results
tools.description: Get per-student outcome results for a course, with optional outcome, student, and alignment filters.

tools.name: get_outcome_rollups
tools.description: Get outcome rollups for a course, optionally aggregated or filtered by students, outcomes, and sort options.

tools.name: get_outcome_contributing_scores
tools.description: Get assignment or quiz scores that contributed to a specific outcome for one or more students in a course.

tools.name: get_outcome_mastery_distribution
tools.description: Get mastery distribution analytics for outcomes in a course, optionally filtered by students or outcomes.

tools.name: get_my_courses
tools.description: List active courses for the authenticated student.

tools.name: get_my_grades
tools.description: Get grade data for the authenticated student. If course_id is omitted, returns grades across all enrolled courses.

tools.name: get_my_submissions
tools.description: List all submissions for the authenticated student in a course.

tools.name: get_my_upcoming_assignments
tools.description: List upcoming assignment events for the authenticated student.

tools.name: get_dashboard_cards
tools.description: Get the current user's dashboard course cards with position, color, and image.

tools.name: get_todo_items
tools.description: Get the current user's to-do items, including upcoming assignments and grading tasks.

tools.name: get_upcoming_events
tools.description: Get the current user's upcoming calendar events and assignments.

tools.name: get_missing_submissions
tools.description: Get assignments with missing submissions for the current user.

tools.name: list_submission_comments_needing_attention
tools.description: List submissions where the most recent comment is from the student and has not been addressed by grading or a reply — i.e. comments the instructor has likely not seen. Returns a triage list, oldest-unaddressed first. Requires instructor/TA permissions in the course.

tools.name: list_students_needing_attention
tools.description: Report students who may need instructor attention based on inactivity, missing or late submissions, and low current score. Each finding lists the exact signals that fired and the thresholds used — this is a factual report, not a prediction. Requires instructor/TA permissions in the course.

tools.name: create_content_export
tools.description: Start a Canvas course content export (Common Cartridge / QTI / zip). Exports are asynchronous — this tool returns immediately with an export ID and initial workflow_state ("created"). Call get_content_export to poll progress and retrieve the time-limited download link when the export finishes.

tools.name: get_content_export
tools.description: Get the status of a content export. When workflow_state is "exported", attachment.url contains a time-limited download link — download it promptly, as the URL expires (re-fetch to get a fresh one). Returns attachment: null while still "created"/"exporting" or on "failed".

tools.name: list_content_exports
tools.description: List all content exports for a course (most recent first).

tools.name: list_grading_standards
tools.description: List grading standards available in a course or account context. Provide either course_id (to see standards scoped to a course) or account_id (to see account-level standards, requires admin access). Returns an array of grading standard objects, each with an id, title, context, and grading_scheme array of { name, value } entries.

tools.name: create_grading_standard
tools.description: Create a new grading standard (letter-to-percentage scheme) in a course or account context. Provide either course_id or account_id (account requires admin). scheme_entries is an array of { name, value } objects where value is the lower-bound percentage as a fraction 0–1 (e.g. { name: "A", value: 0.94 } means A ≥ 94%). Entries will be sorted descending by value before sending to Canvas. Canvas POST body key is grading_scheme_entry (singular); the returned object uses grading_scheme (plural). Returns the created grading standard object including its id — use that id with apply_grading_standard_to_course to activate it on a course.

tools.name: apply_grading_standard_to_course
tools.description: Apply an existing grading standard to a course so the gradebook uses it. Pass the grading_standard_id returned by create_grading_standard or list_grading_standards. Pass null for grading_standard_id to remove the current grading standard from the course. Returns the updated course object.

tools.name: set_student_quiz_accommodation
tools.description: Apply extra time and/or extra attempts to a specific student across all Classic Quizzes in a course (or a specified subset). Fans out to the Canvas quiz extensions API for each quiz. New Quizzes (quiz_type quizzes.next) are skipped — they use a different accommodation mechanism not covered by this tool. Only applies to quizzes that exist at call time; re-run after creating new quizzes. Assignment due-date overrides are not handled here (separate fast-follow feature). Note: for courses with many quizzes this makes one Canvas API call per quiz. Partial failures are tolerated — a failure on one quiz does not abort the rest. Returns the standard fan-out envelope: separated applied[], skipped[] (each with a skip_reason), and failed[] (each with an error) arrays, a not_found list of any requested quiz_ids absent from the course, and a summary of counts. Provide user_id as the real Canvas user ID. If CANVAS_PSEUDONYMIZE_STUDENTS is enabled, call resolve_pseudonym first to obtain the real user_id from a pseudonym.

tools.name: list_student_quiz_accommodations
tools.description: List the current quiz accommodation (extra time and/or extra attempts) for a specific student across all Classic Quizzes in a course. Useful for auditing before or after calling set_student_quiz_accommodation. New Quizzes (quiz_type quizzes.next) are excluded. Reads extra_time / extra_attempts from each quiz's submission records (Canvas exposes no read endpoint for quiz extensions directly) — one Canvas API call per Classic Quiz, so it may be slow for courses with many quizzes. Errors from any quiz's read propagate immediately (no per-quiz error catching). Provide user_id as the real Canvas user ID. If CANVAS_PSEUDONYMIZE_STUDENTS is enabled, call resolve_pseudonym first.

tools.name: list_assignment_overrides
tools.description: List all due-date / availability overrides for a specific assignment in a course. Returns overrides targeting individual students, sections, or groups. Useful for auditing before creating a new override — Canvas returns a 422 if a student-set override already exists for the same students on the same assignment.

tools.name: create_assignment_override
tools.description: Create a due-date / availability override for a specific assignment, targeting a set of students, a course section, or a group. Exactly one of student_ids, course_section_id, or group_id must be provided. At least one date field (due_at, unlock_at, lock_at) should be provided; omit a date field to leave the corresponding date unchanged for the override target. Dates must be ISO 8601 strings (e.g. "2026-09-15T23:59:00Z"). Canvas returns a 422 if a student-set override already exists for the same students on this assignment — use list_assignment_overrides to audit first. Provide student_ids as real Canvas user IDs. If CANVAS_PSEUDONYMIZE_STUDENTS is enabled, call resolve_pseudonym first to resolve pseudonyms to real user IDs.

tools.name: set_student_assignment_dates
tools.description: Fan a due-date / availability override for a specific student across all (or a filtered subset of) assignments in a course. Creates one student-set override per assignment via the Canvas assignment overrides API. Partial failures are tolerated — a failure on one assignment does not abort the rest. Note: for courses with many assignments this makes one Canvas API call per assignment. V1 is create-only: if an override for this student already exists on an assignment, Canvas returns a 422 and that assignment appears in the failed[] list. Use list_assignment_overrides to audit first. Dates must be ISO 8601 strings. To shift dates by a relative amount, first call list_assignments with include=overrides to retrieve current dates, compute absolute timestamps, then call this tool. Returns the standard fan-out envelope: separated applied[], skipped[] (unused in this create-only version, always empty), and failed[] (each with an error) arrays, a not_found list, and a summary of counts. Any assignment_ids that do not exist in the course are reported in not_found (they are neither applied nor failed). Provide user_id as the real Canvas user ID. If CANVAS_PSEUDONYMIZE_STUDENTS is enabled, call resolve_pseudonym first to obtain the real user_id from a pseudonym.

tools.name: check_course_setup
tools.description: Run a factual course-readiness report that surfaces common configuration problems — assignments missing due dates, unpublished items students will not see, gradebook weighting gaps, and graded assignments with no points. Returns findings grouped by check with a plain-language detail per item. This is a config-health report only; it does not inspect student submissions or performance (see list_students_needing_attention / get_missing_submissions for those). Requires instructor permissions in the course.
